title: How to Merge Duplicate Contacts in GoHighLevel — Clean CRM Data
description: Learn how to merge duplicate contacts in GoHighLevel CRM. Step-by-step guide to identify, preview, and safely consolidate duplicates by email, phone, and name.
html_content:
If you've been running your agency or business on GoHighLevel for any length of time, you've probably noticed something: duplicate contacts. They happen. A prospect fills out a form twice. A customer's contact info gets entered manually and synced from an integration. Before you know it, your CRM is cluttered with duplicate records—and that's costing you real money in wasted time, skewed reporting, and confused communication workflows.

The good news? GoHighLevel has a built-in Duplicate Management & Merge Tool designed to help you identify and consolidate duplicates safely and efficiently. In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to use it, best practices for merging without losing critical data, and key restrictions you need to know about.

Ready to clean up your CRM? Get a free 30-day trial of GoHighLevel (double the standard 14-day trial) and experience why thousands of agencies trust one platform to run their entire business.

📋 In This Guide

  • Why Duplicate Contacts Matter (And Cost You Money)
  • How to Access the Duplicate Contact Management Tool
  • Step-by-Step: Merging Duplicate Contacts by Email, Phone, and Name
  • Using Bulk Actions to Merge Multiple Duplicates at Once
  • Preview Before You Merge: The Safe Data Merge Process
  • Key Permissions, Restrictions, and Merge Limits
  • Best Practices for Keeping Your CRM Clean Long-Term

Why Duplicate Contacts Matter (And Cost You Money)

Before we dive into the mechanics, let's talk about the real impact of duplicate contacts. When your CRM is cluttered with duplicates, several things happen:

  • Reporting becomes unreliable. You can't trust your contact counts, pipeline metrics, or conversion data when the same person appears twice in your database.
  • Communication workflows break. Automation sequences fire twice. Follow-up emails get sent to the same person multiple times. Your prospects get annoyed.
  • Time gets wasted. Your team spends cycles chasing duplicate records instead of closing deals or serving clients.
  • Data quality suffers. Incomplete or outdated information from one record might overwrite clean data in another.

That's why taking 30 minutes to merge duplicates can save you hours of headaches down the line. And GoHighLevel's built-in tool makes it straightforward.

How to Access the Duplicate Contact Management Tool

The Duplicate Contact Management & Merge Tool is located directly in your GoHighLevel dashboard. Here's where to find it:

  1. Log in to your GoHighLevel account.
  2. Navigate to Contacts in the left sidebar.
  3. Look for the "Tools" or "Manage" menu within the Contacts section.
  4. Select "Duplicate Contacts" or "Merge Contacts."

Once you're in the Duplicate Management tool, GoHighLevel will scan your database and automatically identify potential duplicates based on matching criteria like email addresses, phone numbers, and full names.

Step-by-Step: Merging Duplicate Contacts by Email, Phone, and Name

GoHighLevel identifies duplicates using three primary match criteria:

1. Email Address Match

This is the most reliable identifier. If two contacts share the exact same email address, GoHighLevel flags them as duplicates. This is usually a straightforward merge—the person filled out a form twice or got added through two different integrations.

2. Phone Number Match

Phone number matching is particularly useful for phone-based businesses or agencies that rely on calling. If two contacts have the same phone number, they're likely the same person. Be careful here—sometimes a business phone number might be shared by multiple people, so verify before merging.

3. Name Match (Fuzzy Matching)

GoHighLevel uses fuzzy matching for names, which catches variations like "John Smith" vs. "Jon Smith" or "Jennifer" vs. "Jenny." Combined with phone or email, name matching is a strong indicator of a duplicate.

The Merge Process:

  1. Review the flagged duplicates. GoHighLevel displays potential duplicates side-by-side for easy comparison.
  2. Select the pair you want to merge.
  3. Designate which record will be the "primary" contact (the one you're keeping).
  4. Review the merged data preview to ensure no critical information is lost.
  5. Click "Confirm Merge" to consolidate the records into a single contact.

💡 Pro Tip

Always take a screenshot or note which contact you're choosing as primary before merging. This helps you track what happened if team members ask about specific contact histories later. GoHighLevel preserves communication history from both contacts, but knowing which "base" record you kept prevents confusion.

Using Bulk Actions to Merge Multiple Duplicates at Once

If you have dozens or hundreds of duplicate contacts (common in scaled agencies), merging them one-by-one would take forever. That's where Bulk Actions come in.

How to Use Bulk Actions for Duplicate Merging:

  1. Go to Contacts in the main menu.
  2. In the Contacts view, use the checkbox column to select multiple contact records (you can select 2-10 contacts per bulk action).
  3. A toolbar will appear. Look for "Bulk Actions" or "More Actions."
  4. Select "Merge Contacts" from the dropdown.
  5. GoHighLevel will display all selected contacts and ask you which one should be the primary contact.
  6. Review the merge preview and confirm.

The beauty of bulk actions is speed. If your duplicate detection tool has flagged 50 obvious duplicates, you can process them in batches of 10 in just a few minutes instead of an hour of one-by-one merging.

Preview Before You Merge: The Safe Data Merge Process

GoHighLevel's merge preview is your safety net. Always review it before hitting confirm.

When you preview a merge, you'll see:

  • Which fields will be retained from the primary contact.
  • Which fields will be overwritten with data from the secondary contact (if applicable).
  • Communication history from both contacts—calls, emails, texts, form submissions—all consolidated into one timeline.
  • Custom fields and pipeline status from both records.

Pay special attention to:

  • Phone number changes. If the two contacts have different phone numbers, verify which one is current before merging.
  • Custom field data. Some custom fields might only have data in one record. Make sure you're not losing important notes or tracking data.
  • Pipeline position. If the contacts are at different stages in your pipeline, confirm which stage should carry forward.

Key Permissions, Restrictions, and Merge Limits

Not everyone on your team should have access to merge contacts. Here's what you need to know about permissions and limits:

Permissions: Only users with "Admin" or "Manager" level access can merge contacts. Team members with lower permission levels can flag duplicates but cannot execute merges. This prevents accidental data loss.

Bulk Action Limits: You can merge a maximum of 10 contacts in a single bulk action. If you need to merge more, run multiple bulk actions in sequence.

Merge Reversibility: Once contacts are merged, the action cannot be undone. This is why the preview step is critical. If you accidentally merge the wrong contacts, you'll need to manually separate them or contact GoHighLevel support.

Automation & Workflows: Merging contacts does not retroactively affect already-completed automations. If a contact ran through a sequence before being merged, those entries remain in the history but won't re-trigger.

Best Practices for Keeping Your CRM Clean Long-Term

Merging duplicates is a one-time cleanup project, but preventing them is the real win. Here's how to stay on top of it:

1. Run Duplicate Detection Monthly

Schedule a monthly check of your duplicate management tool. The sooner you catch and merge duplicates, the less damage they do to your reporting and workflows.

2. Audit Your Integrations

If duplicates spike after connecting a new integration (Zapier, form builder, CRM sync, etc.), audit how that integration maps data. Sometimes a simple field mapping fix prevents dozens of future duplicates.

3. Train Your Team on Contact Entry Best Practices

Most manual duplicates happen when team members create a new contact without checking if one already exists. A simple process—"always search first"—eliminates preventable duplicates.

4. Use Smart Lists to Catch Odd Data Patterns

Create a Smart List that filters contacts with the same phone number or email. Review them quarterly for hidden duplicates that the automatic detection might miss.

5. Set Merge Rules for Automated Processes

If certain integrations or workflows predictably create duplicates, configure those integrations to merge on specific criteria (email match, for example) instead of creating new contacts every time.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I merge contacts across multiple workspaces or locations in GoHighLevel?

No. The merge tool only works within a single workspace or location. If you have contacts duplicated across separate GHL accounts, you'll need to consolidate them into one workspace first or manually manage them separately.

What happens to automation sequences when I merge two contacts?

Active automations will continue to run for the merged contact using the primary record's identity. Completed automation history from both contacts is preserved in the merged record's timeline, but the contact won't re-enter sequences they've already completed.

Does merging duplicate contacts affect my contact count or billing?

Yes and no. Merging reduces your total contact count immediately, which is good for database hygiene. However, GoHighLevel billing is typically based on your plan tier (which includes a contact limit), not per-contact overage. Check your specific plan to confirm how merges affect your billing.

Can I merge contacts that don't have matching emails or phone numbers?

The automatic duplicate detection primarily flags contacts with matching emails, phone numbers, or fuzzy name matches. If you suspect two contacts are duplicates but don't have an obvious match criteria, you can manually merge them through the Contacts list by selecting both and using Bulk Actions—just verify carefully in the preview before confirming.

What data is lost when merging contacts?

Nothing is lost—it's consolidated. All communication history, custom fields, notes, and pipeline activity from both contacts are preserved in the merged record. The secondary contact's record is archived, but its data is incorporated into the primary contact's timeline and field values.
